summary:
Sublingual varices are unrelated to hypertension; they result from different pathophysiological mechanisms than those involving portal hypertension. The available medical literature does not establish any direct or indirect link between these two conditions.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically significant facts related to the query.)

The agent correctly identifies that sublingual varices arise due to distinct vascular pathways—not linked to portal hypertension—and thus have no established association with systemic blood pressure regulation. No credible clinical evidence supports a relationship between sublingual varices and hypertension.
